description_long: This is a large, symmetrical residential house with two prominent wings connected by a central section, all enclosed within a low perimeter wall. The house features two stories with a gable roof on each wing and a connecting flat-roofed section. The front yard is landscaped with evenly spaced trees and shrubs, and a central staircase leads up to the main entrance. The windows are arranged in neat, symmetrical rows, and the facade is simple with minimal ornamentation. The rear of the house includes a partially enclosed area, possibly a garage or patio, and the side yards are also landscaped. The overall appearance is clean and orderly, with a focus on symmetry and functional design.
description: A large, symmetrical two-story house with gable roofs, landscaped front and side yards, and a central staircase entry, all enclosed by a low wall.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, symmetrical wings, central staircase, and landscaped front yard with trees and shrubs.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the gable roof, side yard landscaping, and partial view of the rear enclosed area.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the house, a partially enclosed area (likely a garage or patio), and the symmetry of the structure.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side yard, gable roof, and the connection between the two main wings of the house.
attribute_entryway_design: Central staircase leading to a main door, flanked by landscaped planters and a low wall, with no porch or columns.
